fund: MEGALODOME GOLF Equity Fund I LLC

faqs.q: Isn’t this just an indoor driving range or a simulator venue?
faqs.a: No. It is a real, walkable executive golf course on first-quality artificial turf inside four climate-controlled domes — a 9-hole par-30 you actually play, plus a separate practice facility with technology. Simulators are screens; this is the game. That distinction is the entire category.

No one offers real indoor course play — Topgolf, Five Iron and X-Golf monetize entertainment or practice, not a round of golf. We are positioned as a category creator, protected by trademark and course-design IP rather than a head-to-head feature race.

faqs.q: Why will you win, and stay won?
faqs.a: Municipal approval is secured, the design documentation is complete, and the first-mover plus IP position is hard to copy quickly. The honest risk is first-of-category execution — which is why the team, approvals and a complete model matter as much as the idea.

U.S. off-course golf participation (32.9M) now exceeds on-course play for the first time in history, and Chicago’s outdoor season closes for five to seven months. The model captures that closed-season window at premium winter pricing — peak winter occupancy is modeled near 90%.

A dense, affluent, cold-weather metro about 30 miles west of downtown Chicago, on a 100-acre site with unanimous Village of Oswego approval (Sept 2024). The siting in other materials may be shown as representative; this flagship is the intended first build. The Numbers

faqs.q: How realistic is a 19.7% blended IRR / 2.35x?
faqs.a: It is a modeled base case: a Year-5 exit at 9.0x stabilized EBITDA (~$15.0M), net of debt, with a Year-1 ramp at ~38% realization. It is stress-tested — at a 7.5x exit the blended multiple is ~1.93x. These are targets dependent on the stated assumptions, not guarantees.

The restructured stack carries more debt ($52M), so first-year debt service (~$5.7M) modestly exceeds ramp-year EBITDA (~$5.0M) — a 0.88x coverage. That gap is pre-funded by a 12-month interest reserve sized into the uses of funds; coverage clears 1.4x from FY2028. We disclose this rather than bury it.

9.0x sits at the optimistic end of golf-sector comps, so we model it as the base case and show 7.0x–9.5x in sensitivity. The hard-asset collateral — 100 acres and four domes — underpins value even if the multiple compresses. The Structure — What You Own

faqs.q: What exactly would I be buying?
faqs.a: Three equity routes: Fund (Class B preferred) units and founding-member equity, which together form a $20M investor pool sharing a 10% preferred return; or a direct $5M outside stake for 12.5% of MEGALODOME GOLF CHICAGO WEST INC. The Fund holds 500 Class B Units at $20,000, a 10% preferred return, and 25% of distributable profits; MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC. (the Manager) holds 52.5%. (Final terms per the amended offering documents.)

After a 10% preferred return to the Fund and Membership, exit equity follows ownership: Fund 25%, Membership 10%, Strategic/Outside 12.5%, and MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC. (Manager) 52.5%. Fund and Strategic investors also participate in MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC. as additional upside.

faqs.q: What is my preferred return and when do distributions start?
faqs.a: 10% per annum, cumulative and non-compounded, on the Fund + Membership pool. It accrues during construction (settled later) and begins paying in cash from FY2028 as the asset ramps.

faqs.q: When and how do I get my capital back?
faqs.a: Within a five-year horizon, through refinance after Year 4 or a sale/sale-leaseback at stabilization. Capital is locked for 60 months, at which point two liquidity options apply — convert Fund units to Class A (retaining 25%) or a Company buyout via refinancing; this is an illiquid, long-hold private investment. Risk

faqs.q: What if occupancy lags the model?
faqs.a: Year-1 is stress-tested at 35% / 50% / 70% realization, and the interest reserve covers ramp-year service. Year-1 realization affects ramp-year cash but not the stabilized Year-5 EBITDA that drives exit value.

A 10% construction contingency is built into the uses of funds, design documentation is complete, and Alain Desrochers (CDO) brings 30 years of process-driven development. Cost risk is real and disclosed; the contingency is the first buffer.

faqs.q: What if you can’t refinance or sell at exit?
faqs.a: Exit has three paths — refinance, strategic sale, or sale-leaseback to a triple-net REIT — and the hard real-estate asset provides collateral value independent of any single buyer. Timing and multiple are the key variables, both shown in sensitivity. Team & Process

This is a category-defining first project, so we frame what is true rather than claim realized fund returns: four years of pre-development, unanimous approval, a complete model, and SportsDome’s 15-year school-program operating model that fills weekday off-peak hours. The CFO (Columbia MBA, CFA, ex-Credit Suisse) anchors the financials.

faqs.q: Am I eligible, and what is the minimum?
faqs.a: The offering is limited to accredited investors, whose status is verified. The individual minimum is currently [$100K — under review; raising to $200K would simplify verification]. We will walk you through accreditation as part of subscribing.

A 20-minute discovery call, then a first meeting; if you’re engaged, we open the data room under a mutual NDA for diligence, verify accreditation, and move to subscription documents. Nick Badyal is your single point of contact throughout.

execParas: MEGALODOME GOLF
THE NEXT REVOLUTION™
An institutional summary of the Chicago-West flagship and Fund I offering
Confidential Executive Summary
Flagship Development — Oswego, Illinois (Chicago West)
MEGALODOME GOLF Equity Fund I LLC   ·   Reg D 506(c)   ·   Confidential — For Accredited Investors Only
1 · Fund Strategy & Overview

MEGALODOME GOLF is raising $25 million of equity — across three tranches — alongside $52 million of debt to develop the world’s first fully playable indoor golf course: a real, walkable course inside four interconnected climate-controlled domes that enable 365-day play in any weather. This is not a simulator venue and not a driving range; it is genuine on-course golf, indoors.

The flagship sits on a 100-acre Oswego, Illinois site (≈30 miles west of downtown Chicago) approved unanimously by the Village of Oswego Board in September 2024. The strategy is development-to-stabilization: build a differentiated, recurring-revenue, real-estate-backed asset, stabilize operations, and return capital through refinance or sale within a five-year horizon. The structural tailwind is durable — U.S. off-course golf participation (32.9M) has overtaken on-course play for the first time in history, while cold-climate metros remain structurally underserved for real winter play.
Why This Is a Category, Not a Competitor

Topgolf, Five Iron, X-Golf and traditional dome ranges monetize entertainment or practice — none deliver a real, walkable course indoors. MEGALODOME GOLF is positioned as a category creator, protected by trademark, course-design IP, and the operational expertise to run year-round play.
2 · Capital Structure

Equity arrives at two levels: Fund and Membership units at the fund level, and a direct 12.5% common stake in MEGALODOME GOLF CHICAGO WEST INC. Fund investors hold 500 Class B Units at $20,000, a 10% preferred return, 25% of distributable profits, and additional upside through MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC.; at month 60 two liquidity options apply (convert to Class A at 25%, or buyout via refinancing). Reflects a proposed restructured capital stack. Equity classes, the equity split, the direct 12.5% outside stake, and the distribution waterfall are illustrative and subject to amended offering documents and securities-counsel review before any investor use.
3 · Performance Targets (modeled, not guaranteed)

These are targets, not guarantees, and depend on the assumptions in the accompanying pro-forma — several of which (debt terms, Year-1 occupancy ramp, exit multiple) remain to be finalized and are stress-tested in the model.
4 · Distribution Model — The Waterfall
The Fund uses a whole-fund (European-style) waterfall. Cash flows and exit proceeds are distributed in the following order:
1. The Strategic/Outside investor holds a 12.5% common stake in MEGALODOME GOLF CHICAGO WEST INC., participating in equity value at exit, plus additional upside through MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC.
2. Fund and Membership limited partners receive return of capital.
3. Fund and Membership then receive the 10% cumulative preferred return; unpaid amounts accrue (PIK) and are settled at exit.
4. Above the preferred return, exit equity follows ownership: Fund 25%, Membership 10%, Strategic/Outside 12.5%, and MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC. (Manager) 52.5%.
Illustrative split of exit equity value:
5 · Aligned Incentives & Downside Protection
The 10% cumulative preferred return is the Fund/Membership protected floor; MEGALODOME GOLF USA INC. (the Manager) realizes its 52.5% equity share only after the preferred return is satisfied.
Real-estate-backed asset: a 100-acre site and four domes provide tangible collateral value, not a software promise.
Nine diversified revenue streams reduce single-line volatility; recurring memberships, leagues, and school/college contracts underpin the base.
A 12-month interest reserve and 10% construction contingency are built into uses of funds — sized to cover ramp-year debt service while occupancy stabilizes.
6 · Exit Strategy

Capital is returned within a five-year horizon through one of three mechanisms: (a) refinance of the stabilized asset after Year 4, returning equity while retaining ownership; (b) sale to a strategic golf/hospitality operator or institutional real-estate buyer at a stabilized EBITDA multiple; or (c) sale-leaseback to a triple-net REIT. The base case models a Year-5 exit at 9.0x stabilized EBITDA, net of debt.
7 · Track Record & Operating History

MEGALODOME GOLF is a category-defining first project, so the team frames what is true rather than fabricating realized fund returns. Four years of pre-development work have produced unanimous municipal approval, full design documentation, and a complete financial model. SportsDome Advisory contributes a 15+ year proven school-program operating model (SportsDome Bus, Sport-Étude integration) that fills weekday non-prime hours with institutional revenue — a direct, transferable operating track record.
8 · Team & Governance

Service providers: Vincent Esquire / Timothy Lange (securities counsel) · Huxham Golf Design (course architecture) · Schoppe Design Associates (planning) · SportsDome Advisory (strategy & operating model).
9 · Contact & Next Step

Single point of contact: Nick Badyal, VP Sales & Partnerships. The data room is released in stages. The next step is a 20-minute investor discovery call — link.megalodomegolf.com · invest.megalodomegolf.com/chicago-west. Full model, design documentation, and offering documents follow for qualified accredited investors.

assetPreviewParas: The Chicago-West flagship — a concrete look at what the Fund builds
Chicago-West Flagship · Oswego, Illinois · Representative Development

REPRESENTATIVE FLAGSHIP DEVELOPMENT. This is the Fund’s intended first development. Figures are modeled projections, not guarantees, and actual results will differ. Land control (purchase/option) and debt terms remain to be finalized. Municipal site approval was granted by the Village of Oswego in September 2024.
2 · Development Thesis — Why This Asset, Why Now

The site sits in a dense, affluent, cold-weather metro where outdoor golf closes for 5–7 months — precisely the demand window MEGALODOME GOLF captures at premium winter pricing. Off-course participation has overtaken on-course play nationally, and there is no competing product that lets a Chicago golfer play a real course in January. Municipal approval is already secured, design documentation is complete, and the going-in basis is anchored to hard construction cost ($77.0M all-in) against a stabilized 9.0x EBITDA value of $135.1M — a development-to-stabilization spread that creates the equity.
Develop and open the four-dome flagship and practice facility for a Fall 2027 launch.
Ramp occupancy through Year 1, layering recurring revenue: memberships (six tiers), leagues, and SportsDome school/college contracts that fill weekday non-prime hours.
Stabilize to ~62% EBITDA margin across nine revenue streams by Year 3.
Refinance after Year 4 or sell the stabilized asset, returning investor capital plus the preferred return and each class’s equity share of the upside.

This flagship is the template for the Fund’s broader pipeline: nine facilities over five years across 27 cities, built on a multi-tenant platform (“Megalodome OS”) designed for external licensing from day one. The Chicago-West economics — premium winter pricing, recurring institutional revenue, and a development-to-stabilization value spread — are the model the platform replicates. Concreteness here is intended to make the strategy tangible; it is not a representation that returns are assured.

pitchSlides.title: Slide 1
bullets: The world's first fully playable indoor golf course — real, walkable golf, 365 days a year, in the Chicago metro.
MEGALODOME GOLF Equity Fund  LLC | Confidential

pitchSlides.title: Slide 2
bullets: Golf is a seasonal sport in a year-round market
In two-thirds of the developed world, golf shuts down for five to seven months a year. Winter erases the season. The only "solution" the market has offered is the simulator — a screen, not the game.
No real turf, no walk, no shot you can read off the ground. Serious golfers know the difference — and there is no venue that lets them play an actual course when it's cold.
Every winter, demand exists with nowhere to go.

Confidential — accredited investors only. pitchSlides.title: Slide 3
bullets: The way people play golf has already changed
For the first time in the sport's history, more Americans play golf off-course than on it. Demand has moved indoors — but the product hasn't caught up.
What's missing is a place to play a real course indoors.
Megalodome is built to capture exactly that gap — not with screens, but with genuine, walkable golf under a climate-controlled roof.

pitchSlides.title: Slide 4
bullets: A real golf course — indoors, all year
Four interconnected climate-controlled domes house a genuine executive course, a full practice facility, and a two-story clubhouse. Open 6 AM – 1 AM, 365 days a year, in any weather.
Six par-3s and three par-4s on real turf — a course you walk and play, not a screen.
~110 ft tall, interconnected and climate-controlled for true year-round play.
Full practice facility with short-game area and technology alongside the course.
Two-story clubhouse: sports bar, pro shop, and event space. Fall 2027 opening.

Confidential — accredited investors only. Not an offer to sell or solicitation to buy any security; the offering is made solely through the Confidential Private Placement Memorandum of MEGALODOME GOLF Equity Fund I LLC under Rule 506(c) of Regulation D. Projections are modeled estimates, not guarantees; actual results will differ.

pitchSlides.title: Slide 6
bullets: The right product in the right metro

The flagship sits on 100 acres in Oswego, Illinois — roughly 30 miles west of Chicago, in a dense, affluent, cold-weather market where outdoor golf closes for five to seven months. That closed season is precisely the demand window MEGALODOME GOLF captures at premium winter pricing.
Municipal approval already secured · design documentation complete
Demand peaks exactly when every outdoor course is closed.

275-yard range and 50 stalls, $35–$65 per session, filling daytime and off-peak demand.
F&B, pro shop, banquets, leagues, school/college contracts, and dome naming rights.
